element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to identify and match colored objects in horizontal/vertical/diagonal rows

element_introduction_order.element: Matching mechanic (3+ same color with at least one bomb)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core puzzle loop established; player practices basic arrangements
Direct character control (unique vs. Tetris-style puzzle games); walking on stacks, picking up objects
Advanced object arrangement; spatial problem-solving in 2D field
Some monsters require two quick matches in succession or diagonal matches only
Space constraint increases; player must work faster as playfield shrinks

element_introduction_order.element: Time pressure and field overflow (Game Over condition)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Consequence system; if field fills before clearing, Toad is trapped and game ends
Combo system: 2+ matches chain to stack enemies on opponent's field

typical_relief_triggers: Successful match clearing enemies from field
Toad reaching and manipulating distant object
typical_tension_triggers: Thwomp descending and reducing playfield space
Field filling toward top (Game Over approaching)
Monsters and bombs in scattered, unreachable positions
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty per round; initial monsters increase; spatial constraints tighten via Thwomp; time pressure builds continuously

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core matching mechanic and Toad movement; monsters of single/dual colors; straightforward horizontal/vertical matches
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Special monster types introduced requiring complex match sequences; Thwomp begins appearing; field space constraint increases tension
difficulty_curve.late_game: Combination of special monsters, aggressive Thwomp descents, and dense object packing; time pressure paramount; Game Over risk escalates
notable_spikes: First appearance of Thwomp (spatial constraint shock)
Introduction of special monster types requiring non-standard matches
Each new round increases initial monster count
Late rounds with multiple simultaneous Thwomp descents

notable_level_patterns.description: Each successive round increases the initial number of monsters in the playfield. Player must clear more objects while Thwomp pressure increases. No level-specific layouts mentioned; progression appears arithmetic rather than thematic.

Mid-game introduces monster types with unique destruction requirements (requiring two rapid matches in succession, diagonal-only matches, etc.). These force players to rethink basic matching strategy.
Ceiling descends in waves, compressing available workspace. Forces faster decision-making and reactive stacking. Thwomp disguises as ceiling, adding surprise element.

sources_used: Wikipedia - Wario's Woods (Plot and gameplay section)
The Cutting Room Floor - Wario's Woods (NES) (Gameplay and general structure)
